ucs.title: [LLM] Play (PlayCrypt) Exchange OWASSRF / ProxyNotShell exploitation: w3wp.exe spawning PowerShell/cmd
ucs.description: Hunts for IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning interactive shells, the post-exploitation footprint observed when Play actors weaponize CVE-2022-41080 (OWASSRF) and CVE-2022-41040/41082 (ProxyNotShell) against on-prem Exchange — a hallmark Play initial-access vector documented by CISA AA23-352A and CrowdStrike's PlayCrypt reporting.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_id) as process_id from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(parent_process, "(?i)MSExchange|OutlookSession|autodiscover|owa") OR match(process,"(?i)-enc|FromBase64String|DownloadString|IEX|Invoke-Expression|webclient|hidden|nop\\s") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OutlookSession","autodiscover","OWA","FrontEnd")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","wmic.exe")
| extend SuspiciousFlags = pack_array(
    iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String"), "encoded_ps", ""),
    iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","DownloadFile","WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","Invoke-Expression"), "net_download", ""),
    iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-nop","-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-noni"), "stealth_flag", "")
  )
| where array_length(set_difference(SuspiciousFlags, dynamic([""]))) > 0
   or FileName in~ ("whoami.exe","nltest.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, SuspiciousFlags, ReportId
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Play's signature initial access in 2022-2024 is OWASSRF against unpatched Exchange — CISA's joint advisory and Microsoft's tracking of the cluster (Storm-related) explicitly call out w3wp.exe → PowerShell/cmd as the post-exploit pivot before they drop Cobalt Strike and SystemBC. Legitimate w3wp child-shell activity is vanishingly rare on Exchange servers, so this fires on the actor's actual chokep

ucs.title: [LLM] Play ransomware staging: AdFind + Grixba reconnaissance followed by WinRAR multi-volume exfiltration
ucs.description: Detects Play's distinctive pre-encryption staging: AdFind enumeration of AD, Grixba (.NET info-stealer enumerating AV/EDR/backup software) execution, and chunked exfiltration via WinRAR using the -v split flag with a password — the exact sequence Symantec and CISA attribute to the Balloonfly/Play cluster prior to deploying Playcrypt.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as tools from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("adfind.exe","AdFind.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe","WinRAR.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*-f \"(objectcategory=person)*","*-f objectcategory=computer*","*trustdmp*","*subnets*","*gpodmp*") OR match(Processes.process,"(?i)\\s-(v|m|hp|p)\\d|\\s-v\\d+[mg]\\s|-hp[A-Za-z0-9]{6,}") OR match(Processes.process_name,"(?i)grixba|sysbc|systembc")) by host Processes.user Processes.dest span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval has_adfind=if(match(tools,"(?i)adfind"),1,0), has_archiver=if(match(tools,"(?i)rar|winrar"),1,0), has_grixba=if(match(tools,"(?i)grixba|systembc"),1,0), has_split=if(match(cmdlines,"-v\\d+[mMgG]"),1,0) | eval score=has_adfind+has_archiver+has_grixba+has_split | where score>=2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let window = 4h;
let recon =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "adfind.exe"
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("objectcategory=person","objectcategory=computer","trustdmp","gpodmp","subnets","-sc trustdmp","-sc dcmodes")
    | project ReconTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ReconCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
let stealer =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)(grixba|sysbc|systembc)\.exe"
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Costura","-AVList","-EDRList","-BACKUPList")
    | project StealerTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, StealerCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
let archiver =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","WinRAR.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[mg]\b" 
         or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-hp\S{6,}"
         or ProcessCommandLine has "-m5"
    | project ArchiveTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, ArchiveCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
recon
| join kind=inner archiver on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', ArchiveTime, ReconTime)) <= 240
| join kind=leftouter stealer on DeviceId
| extend ChainScore = 2 + iff(isnotempty(StealerCmd),2,0)
| project FirstSeen = ReconTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ReconCmd, StealerCmd, ArchiveCmd, ChainScore
| sort by ChainScore desc, FirstSeen asc
ucs.rationale: The AdFind → Grixba → WinRAR (-v split, -hp password, -m5) chain inside a four-hour window is the documented Play/Balloonfly pre-encryption playbook (Symantec Threat Hunter report on Grixba, CISA AA23-352A). Grixba is a Play-bespoke .NET tool packed with Costura that enumerates AV/EDR/backup products specifically — joining its execution with AdFind AD recon and the multi-volume RAR splitting Play
